revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出雲大社沖縄分社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Izumo Taisha Okinawa Branch |image = File:Izumo Taisha Okinawa Branch.JPG |location = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|qid=Q181966|lt=Naha|1=Naha}}, Furujima 1-16-13 |coordinates ={{Coord|26.23011111111111|N|127.70352777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |shrine_Rank = |other_names = |festival = * New Year Festival (January 1) * Autumn Festival (November 23) * Tsukishizume-sai (First of every month) |map = Japan Naha city |location = {{ill|Naha|qid=Q181966|1=Naha}} }}
'''Izumo Taisha Okinawa Branch''' (出雲大社沖縄分社, ''Izumo Taisha Okinawa Bunsha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|qid=Q181966|lt=Naha|1=Naha}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an official branch shrine of {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}} and is the southernmost and westernmost branch of Izumo Taisha in Japan.
Within {{ill|Izumo-taishakyo|en|Izumo-taishakyo|qid=Q6102386|lt=Izumo-taisha Kyō|1=Izumo-taishakyo}}, only designations such as “Bunshi” (分祠), “Dai Kyōkai” (大教会, Grand Church/Branch), “Kyōkai” (教会, Church), and “Shikyōkai” (支教会, Sub-Branch) are officially recognized. However, due to the special circumstances following {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, the Okinawa branch was uniquely granted the title of “Bunsha” (分社, Branch Shrine)<ref>[http://www.izumooyashirokyo.or.jp/200_naritachi.html The Origin of Izumo Taisha Kyō] - Izumo Taisha Kyō</ref>.
The main hall is enclosed by {{ill|Concrete block wall|ja|ブロック塀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concrete block wall|1=block wall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11335130}}, making it inaccessible to view. The shrine lacks a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}, and instead, a gate-like structure provides an entrance, with a hand-washing station beneath it. The shrine is particularly popular among young women from across Okinawa, who visit it as a place of worship for romantic blessings{{fact|date=2018-10}}.
== Access == * 4-minute walk from {{ill|Furujima Station|en|Furujima Station|qid=Q7484332|lt=Furujima Station|1=Furujima Station}} on the {{ill|Okinawa Urban Monorail|en|Okinawa Urban Monorail|qid=Q993679|lt=Okinawa Urban Monorail|1=Okinawa Urban Monorail}}.
